Tier 2 optionally reuses the exact on-device Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct model already loaded for Story (js/narrative/ondevice-llm.js) to rephrase Tier 1's facts — never a second model, never an external API — and falls back to Tier 1's unmodified text with no WebGPU. Deliberately holds NO import of and NO call to the Agent Action Firewall's proposeAction/confirmAndApply, so it cannot initiate or complete a data mutation by construction; a structural red-team suite in test/guarded-copilot.test.mjs (34/0 passing) asserts this stays true. Batch 2 wired the read-only chat panel: a 'Copilot' tab (js/app-shell/main.js renderGuardedCopilotTab + #panel-copilot in index.html) that asks/displays only — no write path — and completes the real Tier 2 model call (refineWithOnDeviceModel now actually rephrases via the loaded on-device engine, still falling back to Tier 1 with no WebGPU/model). ENABLED LIVE (flag flipped true) after a live-preview One Confirm gate: the 'Copilot' tab is now in the nav bar for all users on web/desktop/PWA.

zkThresholdProof.description: Zero-Knowledge Threshold Proof (Batch 1): the FIRST genuine zero-knowledge proof primitive in DataGlow (js/provenance/zk-threshold-proof.js), distinct from every other js/provenance/ artifact (selective-disclosure-proof.js, verifiable-check-seal.js, portable-receipt.js, trust-beam.js), which are explicitly documented as NOT zero-knowledge (they commit to a value with a Merkle/SHA-256 tree and then reveal it in cleartext). This module is a non-interactive Schnorr Sigma protocol (Fiat-Shamir heuristic applied to a Pedersen commitment opening) over a deterministically-generated 512-bit safe-prime group (native BigInt modular exponentiation only — zero third-party crypto library, zero WASM, zero new dependency, zero trusted-setup ceremony; group parameters are independently re-verifiable via selfCheckGroup()). Ships ONLY the 'equals zero' predicate this batch: proveZeroCriticalIssues() proves a dataset/query result has ZERO critical (fail-severity) validation flags WITHOUT revealing the actual count, which flags exist, or the underlying data — a genuinely different guarantee than the Verifiable Check Seal's cleartext-disclosure model. Honestly scoped: this is NOT a general range proof (no 'x <= N' for arbitrary N — that needs Bulletproofs-class engineering, a documented future batch, not implied to exist here), and the 512-bit group is sized for instant in-browser verification on any device including a phone, not maximum long-term security against a nation-state-level adversary (disclosed explicitly in the module's own ZK_PROOF_DISCLAIMER). If the statement is false (there ARE critical flags), proveZeroCriticalIssues() honestly REFUSES to fabricate a proof rather than lying. Wired as an opt-in 'Prove zero critical issues' button in the SQL tab's Local Analysis Contract flow (js/app-shell/main.js renderZkThresholdProofAffordance), alongside but independent of the existing Verifiable Check Seal button. Ships DARK (land-dark per the Build Nervous System convention): a human must click the button, nothing proves automatically, and with this flag off (its shipped default) no proof UI renders and the SQL tab behaves exactly as before this PR. 31/31 tests pass (test/zk-threshold-proof.test.mjs) covering completeness, soundness (tampered response/commitment/label/kind all correctly fail), and zero-knowledge (the secret blinding factor is never present in the serialized artifact).

queryVerificationSentinel.description: Query Sentinel (Batch 1): a per-query, deterministic static analyzer for the SQL tab distinct from the whole-dataset Local Analysis Contract (js/validation/analysis-contract.js). js/validation/query-sentinel.js checks four silent-bug classes on the query just run: FANOUT (a non-unique joined-side key before an aggregate can multiply rows and inflate a result), JOIN_KEY (a declared column-type mismatch across a JOIN ON condition), ADDITIVITY (a GROUP BY on a column that resolves to a non-unique joined-table column, so per-group totals won't sum to the ungrouped total), and SENSITIVE_COLUMN (delegates entirely to the existing classifySensitiveColumns/isSensitiveCategory predicate in js/agents/phi-prompt-guard.js — no new pattern list). Pure logic, no DOM/DuckDB/network import, and degrades gracefully to a single info-severity hint rather than a false fail when a table's row count or a column's cardinality wasn't measured for the run — the same graceful-degradation convention Local Analysis Contract uses. 22/22 tests pass (test/query-sentinel.test.mjs). Ships DARK: wired into the SQL tab's query-run path as its own independent branch with its own dismissible card; with this flag off (its shipped default) nothing imports the module and the existing SQL tab flow is byte-for-byte unchanged.

querySentinelAssist.description: Query Sentinel Assist (Batch 2 of 3): an opt-in, bounded on-device assist layered on top of Query Sentinel's (Batch 1, queryVerificationSentinel) already-computed FANOUT/JOIN_KEY/ADDITIVITY/SENSITIVE_COLUMN flags. Adds one button to the Query Sentinel card, 'Explain & suggest a fix'. Tier 1 (js/validation/query-sentinel-assist.js's assistDeterministic + buildFixSuggestion) is a zero-model, zero-cost, fully template-based per-rule-class fix-sketch lookup — every possible output string is visible by reading the file, and it can never report a finding Batch 1 didn't already report. Tier 2 (assistWithOnDeviceModel) reuses the EXACT same on-device model js/narrative/ondevice-llm.js already loads for Story and Guarded Copilot (Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct via WebLLM) — no second model, no new WebGPU path — purely to rephrase Tier 1's text into more natural language under a narrow system prompt that forbids inventing any new finding, severity, or fix idea; it falls back to the exact Tier 1 text whenever WebGPU/the model isn't available, matching Guarded Copilot's own Tier 2 fallback contract byte-for-byte. Architecturally incapable of writing/executing anything: no import of the DuckDB engine, no import of agent-action-firewall's confirmAndApply, no write/insert/delete/update/mutate/execute call anywhere in the file — proven by a red-team structural test, not just by convention. 30/30 tests pass (test/query-sentinel-assist.test.mjs). Ships DARK and fully independent of queryVerificationSentinel's own flag: with this flag off (its shipped default) the assist button never renders and nothing imports query-sentinel-assist.js, so the SQL tab and the Batch 1 card are byte-for-byte unchanged.

querySentinelBridge.description: Query Sentinel Bridge (Batch 3 of 3, final): resolves `FROM py.<name>` / `FROM r.<name>` cross-runtime table references in the SQL tab against the Polyglot Workbench's Object Space registry (js/app-shell/object-space.js, Batch B) — the working version of what that module's own header explicitly says it does NOT yet do ('no working FROM py.name yet'). js/validation/query-sentinel-bridge.js is a pure text-transform resolver: given the SQL string and the live listObjectSpace() array, it rewrites every exact `py:<name>` / `r:<name>` registry match to the real underlying DuckDB table (via each entry's provenance field) and returns an explicit resolved/unresolved list — a near-miss or never-loaded name is left completely untouched (never a fuzzy/partial-match guess) and surfaced as an honest 'could not find X — run that tab first' message, never a silent wrong-table substitution. HONESTLY SCOPED (confirmed by direct inspection of registerRuntimeObjects() and both language bridges): this only resolves references to datasets ALREADY LOADED and registered from a prior SQL/Python/R run — it does NOT capture a brand-new, one-off variable a Python or R script computed inline that was never one of the app's loaded datasets; true arbitrary-variable capture is an explicit future batch, not implied to exist here. Wired into runSqlQuery() as a resolution pass immediately before the existing @metric expansion step — when this flag is off (its shipped default) the resolver is never called and the SQL tab's query-run path is byte-for-byte unchanged; when on, a query with no py./r. reference at all also runs byte-for-byte unchanged (the resolver is a no-op pass-through). Architecturally incapable of writing/executing anything itself: no import of the DuckDB engine, no runQuery/createTableFromRows call, no write/insert/delete/update/mutate/execute call anywhere in the file — the caller (main.js) still owns handing the rewritten SQL to the exact same engine.runQuery() path every other query already goes through. 31/31 tests pass (test/query-sentinel-bridge.test.mjs), including a red-team structural pass proving the no-DuckDB-import and exact-match-only guarantees. Ships DARK and fully independent of queryVerificationSentinel's and querySentinelAssist's own flags.

polyglotAutocomplete.description: Polyglot Workbench Batch D: schema-aware inline suggestions for the SQL, Python, and R editors (js/polyglot/polyglot-autocomplete.js). Suggestion pools are built from the live Object Space registry — table names, column names (SQL: bare; Python: bracket notation; R: $ notation), and language-specific snippets (SQL keywords/functions, Python pandas fragments, R dplyr fragments). getSuggestions() returns scored, deduplicated suggestions; topSuggestion() returns the single best ghost-text suffix. Pure: no DOM, no DuckDB, no network. 40/40 tests.

polyglotErrorAdvisor.description: Polyglot Workbench Batch E: cross-language error messages with a grounded Suggested fix (js/polyglot/polyglot-error-advisor.js). Parses SQL/Python/R error strings into structured kind+detail+hint, then cross-references the Object Space registry to add a suggestedFix when the named identifier resolves to an object in a different language — e.g. querying a Python-registered table in SQL suggests FROM py.name; referencing a SQL table in Python suggests dataglow.get_df(). Never fabricates a fix for an identifier not in the registry. renderAdvisedErrorHtml() produces an HTML snippet matching the existing formatSqlError/renderSqlErrorHtml shape. XSS-safe. 44/44 tests.

rigorEngineBadges.description: The Rigor Engine, Batch 2 of 4 (per NORTH_STAR.md's plan): honest per-group statistical confidence badges, plus the SQL→Visualize gap fix. js/rigor/statistical-rigor.js gains two new pure functions — classifyGroupedConfidence(rows, groupCol, valueCol) runs the existing Batch-1 classifyConfidence() over EVERY distinct group in a GROUP-BY-shaped result (never a single verdict for a mixed result set), and summarizeGroupedConfidence() folds that into one conservative verdict for a compact badge by taking the WORST group's verdict, never an average. Two render surfaces share the exact same badge card (renderRigorBadgeCard in main.js): (1) the SQL tab auto-detects a categorical+numeric column pair in any query result and badges it via renderRigorConfidenceBadge(), and (2) the Visualize tab badges the currently-charted x/y pair the same way via renderVisualizeRigorBadge() whenever x is categorical, y is numeric, and the chart type isn't scatter (which has no grouping concept). Separately, this batch fixes a real gap found during investigation: populateVisualizeBuilder()/viz.renderChart() could ONLY ever read getActiveDataset() — a SQL query's own result set had no path into a chart at all. renderSendToVisualizeAffordance() adds a 'Send to Visualize' button under every non-empty SQL result that registers the result as a real DuckDB table via the exact same engine.createTableFromRows/getTableSchema/addDataset sequence the existing Red Team v2 synthetic-dataset flow already uses, switches to Visualize, and pre-selects x/y from the same detection heuristic the badge uses. Every new code path is gated behind this flag: with it off (its shipped default) renderRigorConfidenceBadge/renderSendToVisualizeAffordance/renderVisualizeRigorBadge all return immediately on their first line, so the SQL tab, Visualize tab, and every existing badge/card render byte-for-byte unchanged. 56/56 tests pass (test/statistical-rigor.test.mjs, extended with 17 new cases for the two new functions plus a source-scan proving the module still names no DOM/network/DuckDB primitive).

temporalOrderChecks.description: Phase 2 — Temporal Order Checker. Detects physically impossible event orderings within a single table (discharge before admit, lab result before order, payment before claim, death before birth, prior auth after service). Auto-detects date column pairs by name heuristic. Hard rule violations fail at any rate; soft rules use 0.1%/1% thresholds. Results in validationResults.relational.temporal.

flagConsistencyChecks.description: Phase 2 — Flag Consistency Checker. Detects logical contradictions between binary flag columns. Built-in rules: readmit_30d=1 but readmit_90d=0, readmit_7d=1 but readmit_30d=0, inpatient=1 AND outpatient=1, emergency_admit=1 AND elective_admit=1, deceased=1 but discharge_disposition not expired (soft). Only runs rules whose requiredCols are all present. Supports custom extraRules.

joinCoverageChecks.description: Phase 2 — Join Coverage Checker. Measures child-side join coverage rate (% of child rows that match a parent key). Thresholds: >=99% pass, 95-99% warn, 75-95% fail medium, <75% fail high. Complements FK orphan check. Stored in validationResults.relational.joinCoverage.

equityStratification.description: Phase 3 -- Equity Stratification Layer. Auto-detects race/ethnicity, sex/gender, zip/geography, payer class, age group, and disability/dual columns. Stratifies outcome metrics (readmission, denial, mortality, LOS, cost, ED utilization, quality score) by group using DuckDB GROUP BY queries. Scores disparities against CMS Disparities Impact Statement thresholds (rate ratio >= 1.5x or abs diff >= 5 pp = fail; 1.25x / 3 pp = warn). Builds a signed equity attestation block embedded in the Trust Certificate. Gated by this flag. Results in validationResults.equity. Fails open: any error produces idle status.

temporalDrift.description: Phase 4 -- Temporal Drift Layer. Computes freshness decay (trust score multiplier based on data age + rulepack stale/expiry thresholds), captures a statistical snapshot (row count, null rates, numeric means per column), and diffs against a prior snapshot when options.priorSnapshot is provided. Version-pins the active rulepack. Results in validationResults.temporal_drift. Fails open.

rulepacks.description: Phase 4 -- Versioned Rulepacks. All domain-specific thresholds (equity disparity, FK orphan rates, temporal order, join coverage, freshness decay) now come from a named rulepack rather than hardcoded constants. Built-in packs: healthcare (CMS/NCHS thresholds), general (domain-agnostic defaults). Custom packs can be registered at runtime via registerPack(). The active pack is version-pinned in the Trust Certificate.

joinBuilder.description: Phase 8 -- Multi-Table Visual Join Builder. Adds a "Join Builder" tab with an SVG drag-and-drop canvas. Users select tables from their loaded datasets, add them to the canvas as schema cards, draw join lines between columns (click-to-connect), change join type (INNER/LEFT/RIGHT/FULL) by clicking an edge, and generate production-ready DuckDB SQL automatically. The Run query button pushes the generated SQL into the SQL tab and executes it. Ships enabled (not dark) because the feature is additive and has no risk to existing tabs.

nlSql.description: Phase 9 -- NL→SQL via Schema-Only + Metric Contracts. Adds an "Ask AI" tab where the user types a question in plain English and receives a DuckDB SQL SELECT statement. PRIVACY: only column names and types are sent to the LLM (never row data or patient records). Named metrics (readmission rate, denial rate, LOS, etc.) are resolved via Metric Contracts -- pinned SQL expressions that bypass LLM freestyle and produce reproducible, auditable SQL. Provider and API key configured per-session in the UI. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, and Perplexity. Ships enabled; the tab only shows when a provider and API key are configured.

dataVersionControl.description: Phase 10 -- Data Version Control. Adds a Versions tab where users can snapshot datasets (schema + stats only, no row data), diff any two snapshots side-by-side (schema changes + statistical drift detection with BREAKING/WARN/OK risk levels), and get advisory rollback info. Export/import snapshots as portable JSON. Snapshot fingerprinting prevents duplicate captures.

aiCouncil.description: Phase 11 -- Multi-Model AI Council. Adds a Council tab where the user asks one analytical question and gets answers from three LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini) called in parallel. The Council then synthesizes the results: CONSENSUS where all three agree, MAJORITY where two of three agree, and CONTESTED where all three differ, plus an overall agreement rating. Primary use case is data questions (for example: which metric best explains readmission risk, or what SQL computes denial rate) -- not general chat. PRIVACY: schema (column names + types) only is sent to each provider, the same guarantee as NL->SQL -- never row data or patient records. Provider and API keys are configured per-session in the UI and held in page memory only, never stored. Users can run the Council with 1, 2, or 3 providers enabled; synthesis only runs when at least 2 providers respond successfully.

serverOffload.description: Server offload (opt-in ONLY). When enabled AND a valid endpoint URL is configured, heavy queries can be delegated to a remote DuckDB instance. NEVER enabled by default. DataGlow is local-first. See js/app-shell/duckdb-config.js #8.

mcpInterface.description: AI Readiness Gate Batch 4 (MCP interface): exposes DataGlow's validation gate to any external AI agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf) via the Model Context Protocol. Ships as a Node.js stdio MCP server (js/mcp/dataglow-mcp-server.mjs) that wraps the already-tested gate logic from Batch 1 (readiness-gate.js) and Batch 3 (agent-gate.js). Exposes: one tool (check_readiness -- returns agentConsumable verdict, score, failingLayers, reasons), two resource types per dataset (dataglow://schema/{name} and dataglow://validation/{name}), and two prompt templates (analyze_validated_dataset, fix_failing_layers). Reads a dataglow-gate-state.json file written by the browser app via js/mcp/gate-state-exporter.js when the user exports their gate result. Zero new validation logic -- the MCP layer is a thin typed adapter. Ships OFF: this flag gates the Settings tab Export Gate State button that triggers the file write; the MCP server process itself is always available to run manually via node js/mcp/dataglow-mcp-server.mjs regardless of flag state.

agentPassportBridge.description: Agent Passport Bridge: adds a new get_agent_passport MCP tool that composes FOUR already-live DataGlow signals into one JSON trust object for external MCP-calling agents (Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP client) -- the AI Readiness Gate verdict (readiness-gate.js, unchanged), Semantic Layer metric-contract status (ds.metricContractStatus as already exported, semantic-layer.js), AI Touch Ledger history/chain-verification (ai-touch-ledger.js's existing summarizeTouchLedger/verifyTouchLedger, now also carried through the gate-state export), and Verifiable Check Seal status (verifiable-check-seal.js's existing sealCheckResult output, the Proof Room tab's already-minted seal, also now carried through the export). Zero new checks, zero new crypto, zero new scoring -- every field is read verbatim or with a trivial pass-through mapping. Extends js/mcp/gate-state-exporter.js's buildGateStatePayload/serializeGateState with an optional, backward-compatible extras param (touchLedgerSummary, proofRoomSeal) and extends the Settings tab's Export Gate State button (main.js) to populate them from the browser's existing global AI Touch Ledger and Proof Room seal. Any missing signal is reported honestly (checked:false / available:false / sealed:false) rather than defaulted to a pass. Depends on the mcpInterface flag also being on (same Settings tab export gate). Ships OFF: with this flag off, get_agent_passport is still registered in ListToolsRequestSchema (matching check_readiness's own always-registered pattern) but is a net-new, additive tool -- no existing tool, resource, or prompt behavior changes. 81/81 tests in test/mcp-server.test.mjs (59 pre-existing + 22 new).

dataDiplomacyLoader.description: Data Diplomacy Batch 3: replaces the hardcoded two-claim demo scenario with a real data-loading UI (js/diplomacy/diplomacy-loader.js). Users pick any loaded dataset, choose entity-ID and value columns, enter the entity ID value to compare, and set source label + confidence + signer ID per party. The Reconcile button is enabled only when both claim forms are complete. The loader queries DuckDB to read the actual cell value for each party's row, then seals and reconciles using the same Batch-1 engine. The Batch-2 two-key approval UX renders below the forms after reconciliation completes. 25/25 tests. Paired with the top-level dataDiplomacy flag.

dataDiplomacyP2P.description: Data Diplomacy Batch 4: sealed-claim peer-to-peer exchange adapter (js/diplomacy/diplomacy-p2p-transport.js). DIPLOMACY_CLAIM_MESSAGE_KIND='diplomacy-claim'; buildClaimMessage()/isValidClaimMessage() are pure wire-format helpers; createDiplomacyP2PTransport({transport, selfId}) wraps any injected broadcast transport (typically a RoomBroadcastCoordinator) with sendClaim(claim)->Promise<boolean> and onReceiveClaim(fn)->unsubscribe(). NULL_DIPLOMACY_TRANSPORT is the no-op fallback when no Rooms session is active. Follows the exact dependency-injection discipline proven by federated-transport.js, room-signaling.js, and room-broadcast.js: unknown message kinds are silently ignored, handler errors never abort other handlers, destroy() tears down the subscription. Wired into renderDiplomacyTab in main.js when both dataDiplomacy and dataDiplomacyP2P flags are on: the loader form gains a 'Share claim with peer' button that calls sendClaim() with the locally sealed claim; onReceiveClaim() fills the other party's form from the incoming claim. Ships DARK: with the flag off the transport is never constructed and the Diplomacy tab is byte-for-byte unchanged. 30/30 tests in test/diplomacy-p2p-transport.test.mjs.

liveRoomsBroadcast.description: Live Rooms Batch 2: broadcast action items to peers via Rooms WebRTC transport. New pure adapter js/agents/live-rooms-broadcast.js adds a 'live-action-items' message kind on top of the existing Rooms data channel. LIVE_ACTION_ITEMS_MESSAGE_KIND='live-action-items'; buildActionItemsMessage({actionItems, meetingId, from})/isValidActionItemsMessage(msg) are pure wire-format helpers (build returns null for an empty items list; isValid requires the correct kind AND a non-null actionItems array); createLiveRoomsBroadcast({transport, selfId}) wraps any injected broadcast transport (the send(msg)/onReceive(fn) shape RoomBroadcastCoordinator uses) with broadcastActionItems(actionItems, meetingId)->Promise<boolean> (returns false on any failure, never throws) and onReceiveActionItems(fn)->unsubscribe() (handler errors never abort other handlers). NULL_LIVE_ROOMS_BROADCAST is the no-op fallback when no Rooms session is active. Follows the exact dependency-injection discipline proven by diplomacy-p2p-transport.js and room-broadcast.js. Wired into renderMeetingScribeTab in main.js when liveRoomsBroadcast is on and window.__dataglow_rooms_broadcast exists: the local grounded action-item list is broadcast to peers, and incoming remote items are merged into the local list and re-rendered so watchers see the list update live on their own device. Ships DARK: with the flag off the transport is never constructed and the Meeting tab is byte-for-byte unchanged. 35/35 tests in test/live-rooms-broadcast.test.mjs.

chartContextTimeline.description: Live Rooms Batch 3: real chart-context timeline wiring. New pure, Node-testable module js/agents/chart-context-timeline.js records which chart/query the analyst was viewing when each meeting line was spoken, so the Meeting Scribe feeds tagSegmentsWithContext a REAL timeline instead of an empty array (every tag was null before). buildChartContextEntry({chart, queryLabel, ts}) is a pure helper (chart must be a non-empty string, queryLabel defaults to null, ts defaults to Date.now(), returns null on bad input, never throws). createChartContextTimeline() returns recordChartView({chart, queryLabel, ts}) (silent no-op on bad input, never throws), getTimeline() (a frozen, safe copy — mutating it never changes internal state), and clear(). Wired into main.js: when the flag is on, the SQL result render path calls recordChartView with the query text truncated to 60 chars, and renderMeetingScribeTab supplies chartContextTimeline.getTimeline() to mountMeetingScribe via a getContextTimeline option that the scribe passes to tagSegmentsWithContext. Ships DARK: with the flag off the singleton is never created, the scribe uses its default [] and every existing path is byte-for-byte unchanged. 26/26 tests in test/chart-context-timeline.test.mjs.

meetingSynthesis.description: Live Rooms Batch 4: on-device LLM synthesis panel for the captured meeting. New pure, Node-testable module js/agents/meeting-synthesis.js turns a grounded meeting note (the output of buildMeetingNote in js/agents/meeting-scribe-agent.js) into a focused prompt for the SAME on-device model the Story and Guarded Copilot tabs use, then packages the result. buildSynthesisPrompt(meetingNote) returns {systemPrompt, userPrompt} (both plain-language strings, always valid even for a null or empty note; the system prompt forbids inventing owners, dates, or numbers and asks for a four-part summary: pushback moments, data requests, action items, key grounding context). summarizeMeetingSynthesis(rawResponse, meetingNote) returns {summary, actionItemCount, pushbackCount, contextReferenceCount}, trimming the model text and drawing the counts from the note; never throws. isSynthesisAvailable() is a pure stub returning false, DI-wired in main.js to a WebGPU check (isOnDeviceSynthesisAvailable). Wired into the Meeting tab behind this flag via a separate host (#meeting-synthesis-body): a 'Synthesize with on-device model' button loads the model if needed (exact Story-tab progress + cancel pattern), runs buildSynthesisPrompt(currentMeetingNote) through the engine, and displays the summary plus grounding counts. Ships DARK: with the flag off the host stays empty and the Meeting tab is byte-for-byte unchanged. 26/26 tests in test/meeting-synthesis.test.mjs.

pivotTable.description: Pivot Table Batch 1: an Excel-style pivot builder answering the direct 'no pivot table UI exists' gap found this run. New pure, Node-testable module js/pivot/pivot-builder.js (zero DOM/browser globals) generates real DuckDB SQL from a {sourceTable, rows, columns, values} config -- buildGroupBySQL() for a Rows-only summary, buildPivotSQL() for a real DuckDB PIVOT ... ON ... USING ... GROUP BY cross-tab when a Columns well is populated, buildPivotQuery() as the single dispatch+validate entry point, plus classifyColumns()/validateConfig()/quoteIdent()/buildCardinalityCheckSQL() (a pre-flight distinct-combination count capped at MAX_PIVOT_CARDINALITY=200 to refuse silently generating a pivot with an unusable number of result columns). Deliberately reuses the existing DuckDB-WASM engine instead of a second, parallel JS aggregation implementation, so pivot numbers are provably the same numbers the SQL tab would produce for an equivalent hand-written query -- verified directly: 27/27 tests in test/pivot-builder.test.mjs run real generated SQL against a real in-test DuckDB table (not string-matching), including multi-measure Values, COUNT(col) NULL-handling, and quoted space-containing column names. The thin browser renderer js/runtimes-viz/pivot-ui.js owns the Rows/Columns/Values well UI -- a tap-to-add chip picker (never native HTML5 drag-and-drop, since DataGlow is mobile-primary and drag-and-drop is a poor touch fit), a Run Pivot button, and a result table reusing the same .result-table CSS the SQL tab uses. Wired into main.js behind this flag: a new 'pivot' tab id in TAB_META/state.tabOrder, gated in getVisibleTabIds() (the single source of truth per the code's own 'any new dark-flag tab only needs to be added HERE' comment), and a switchTab hook calling pivotUi.renderPivotTab(). Ships DARK: with the flag off the 'pivot' tab is never added to the bar, is never a dead click target, #panel-pivot stays empty, and every existing tab/module is byte-for-byte unchanged.

narrativeOverconfidenceGuard.description: Closes the gap named directly by the Stanford HAI 'delusional spirals' article (sycophancy/overconfidence in generated text): the Rigor Engine (all 4 batches complete) and story.js's buildStoryClaims() already grade every quantitative claim A-D and story.js's own generateLocalStory()/ondevice-llm.js's STORY_SYSTEM_PROMPT already INSTRUCT a model to hedge grade C/D claims -- but nothing previously verified the model's actual generated text obeyed that instruction. New pure, Node-testable module js/rigor/narrative-overconfidence-guard.js (zero DOM/network/model dependency) exports checkNarrativeOverconfidence(text, claims) -> {status: 'pass'|'warn'|'idle', findings}, which splits the narrative into sentences, locates the sentence(s) relevant to each claim (falling back to a whole-text scan for paraphrased claims, honestly reporting sentence:null when it can't localize), and for every grade C/D claim only (grade A/B claims are never flagged regardless of wording) checks two things: (1) overconfident language ('clearly', 'definitely', 'proves', 'always', 'never', '100% certain', etc.) attached to that weak claim, and (2) a total absence of hedge/caveat language anywhere in the narrative for that claim (the hedge check deliberately scans the WHOLE narrative, not just the claim-relevant sentence, since a real caveat sentence like story.js's own 'Treat this average cautiously -- it rests on limited or partly-missing data.' legitimately doesn't repeat the claim's number). describeOverconfidenceFinding() renders a plain-language line per finding. 29/29 tests in test/narrative-overconfidence-guard.test.mjs, including a source scan proving no DOM/network/DuckDB/model primitive is named. Wired into the Story tab in main.js: renderNarrativeOverconfidencePanel(storyText, queryResult) is called once, right after renderStoryClaims(), into a new #story-overconfidence-guard card sitting directly below the existing Narrative Consistency Check card -- reusing buildStoryClaims() so the guard checks the exact same claims/grades the visible per-claim badges already show, never a second independent scoring pass. Ships DARK: with the flag off, renderNarrativeOverconfidencePanel() returns on its first line before touching the DOM or re-deriving claims, #story-overconfidence-guard stays empty and display:none, and every existing Story tab render path is byte-for-byte unchanged.

portfolioNarrativeAssembler.description: Answers the analyst's own ask directly: can a full DataGlow session become one shareable write-up for a portfolio site, LinkedIn post, or resume attachment -- without re-deriving any numbers. New pure, Node-testable module js/portfolio/narrative-assembler.js (zero DOM/DuckDB/network dependency) exports buildCleaningSummary(issues, auditLog), buildNarrativeDocument({problemFramerMarkdown, storyDoc, issues, auditLog, recommendation, overconfidenceFindings, options}), renderNarrativeMarkdown(doc), and validateNarrativeDocument(doc). Stitches four things that already exist as separate modules: (1) Problem Framer's export markdown (js/problem-framing/problem-framer.js's buildExportMarkdown()), (2) the Story tab's narrative text, (3) a Clean tab issue/fix summary (built from js/cleaning/clean.js's scanForIssues() output plus the session's applied-fix audit log -- resolution is inferred from either a structured {issueId} entry or, matching DataGlow's real live audit-log shape, a quoted column name inside a freeform fix-description string), and (4) an analyst-authored closing recommendation, plus an optional Confidence Caveats section when the Narrative Overconfidence Guard has already flagged findings for the same story text. Fail-open throughout: never throws on missing/malformed input, always produces all 4 required section types so validateNarrativeDocument() always passes even with zero real input. 57/57 tests in test/portfolio-narrative-assembler.test.mjs, including live-app-shape audit-log strings and raw (no-status) narrative-overconfidence-guard.js finding objects. Wired into a new 'Portfolio' tab via js/portfolio/portfolio-ui.js (thin renderer, zero business logic): reads Problem Framer's live #framer-intake/[data-framer-q] inputs, state.lastStory, window.__dataglowLastCleanIssues + window.__dataglowAuditLog (both newly captured at their existing Clean-tab call sites), and window.__dataglowLastOverconfidence (newly captured alongside the existing Story tab's own overconfidence panel render) -- never re-running any of those four checks itself. Ships DARK: with the flag off, the 'portfolio' tab is filtered out of getVisibleTabIds() exactly like 'pivot', never added to the bar, never a dead click target, and #panel-portfolio stays empty; every existing tab's render path (including the two new window.__dataglow* capture lines, which are pure assignments with no conditional logic) is otherwise byte-for-byte unchanged.

sentinelGateway.description: Sentinel Local Agent Gateway -- typed, governed MCP-style interface to local DuckDB data with query budget enforcement and tamper-evident audit log

storageWatchdog.description: Cross-platform storage durability watchdog -- iOS ITP eviction warnings, Android Auto Backup risk notices, Tauri at-rest encryption guidance

offlineAICopilot.description: Sentinel Phase 3 -- Offline AI Copilot toggle. Routes NL queries through local WebGPU/WebLLM engine (Qwen2.5-Coder-3B) when on, falls back to WASM rule engine. Zero data egress on both paths.

securityAdvisor.description: Sentinel 3.5 -- SecurityAdvisor: Layer 1 regex threat model on SQL/Python/R before execution + proof chain integrity checks. All local, zero API calls. Surfaces findings in Sentinel governance panel.
